macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Rebound After Initial Gap-Down; Month-End Flows Expected
items.summary: US indexes including Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq gapped lower but rebounded, with Dow dipping -1.78% amid US embassy security concerns before recovering. Chicago PMI data confirms strong US economic performance, supporting outlook amid month-end flows.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Futures Weaker in Sideways Grind at Lower Levels
items.summary: March bitcoin futures prices weakened in early U.S. trading on February 27, entering a sideways grind at lower levels amid subdued market action.

items.title: Bitcoin Rally Stalls Amid Mass Layoffs and AI Uncertainty
items.summary: Bitcoin faces pressure as its rally stalls due to surging layoffs in major corporations and growing anxiety over artificial intelligence disruptions, draining liquidity from risk assets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Perplexity Launches Perplexity Computer for Long-Running Autonomous AI Workflows
items.summary: Perplexity introduced Perplexity Computer, a multi-model platform orchestrating 19 specialized AI models to execute complex workflows lasting hours or months, breaking objectives into subtasks with sub-agents, APIs, and file system access.

items.title: Microsoft Previews Copilot Tasks as Cloud-Based Background AI Agent
items.summary: Microsoft unveiled Copilot Tasks, a cloud-hosted AI system for independent execution of recurring or one-time tasks described in natural language, handling scheduling, monitoring, and drafting while seeking approvals for key actions.

items.title: Multiverse Computing Releases Compressed HyperNova 60B Frontier Model
items.summary: Spanish startup Multiverse Computing launched a compressed version of its HyperNova 60B model using CompactifAI technology, halving size while maintaining accuracy, reducing memory and latency, and improving tool calling for agentic tasks.

items.title: Alphabet Integrates Robotics Firm Intrinsic into Google AI Operations
items.summary: Alphabet is bringing robotics software company Intrinsic closer to Google operations, collaborating with Google DeepMind using Gemini models and Cloud infrastructure to advance industrial robotics and physical AI.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Code Automates COBOL Modernization, Tanks IBM Shares 13%
items.summary: Anthropic announced Claude Code for automating COBOL code analysis and modernization, mapping dependencies across thousands of lines; IBM shares dropped 13% amid fears of disruption to its mainframe consulting business.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Integrates Gemini 3 with Full Agentic Capabilities
items.summary: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy S26 series featuring Google Gemini 3 for autonomous app operations like Uber, blending it with Perplexity and Bixby, targeting 800 million Gemini-powered devices by year-end.

items.title: VAST Data Unveils Platform Updates to Collapse AI Data Stack at VAST Forward
items.summary: VAST Data announced Policy Engine, Tuning Engine, Polaris, and NVIDIA partnerships to embed vector acceleration, automate RLHF, and streamline AI infrastructure, reducing reliance on external databases and ETL processes.

items.title: Agent World Pipeline Generates 1,000 Synthetic Environments for Faster AI Training
items.summary: Researchers introduced Agent World, creating 1,000 diverse synthetic environments with databases for reliable agent training, achieving strong generalization on benchmarks and surpassing traditional methods.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Netflix Abandons Warner Bros. Games Acquisition
items.summary: Netflix appears to have backed out of acquiring Warner Bros. Games, following Warner Bros. Discovery's scheduled special meeting on March 20, 2026, for approval that now seems unlikely to proceed.

items.title: Pokémon Unveils New Title for 30th Anniversary
items.summary: The Pokémon Company marked its 30th anniversary with a special Pokémon Presents presentation on February 27, 2026, unveiling Pokémon Wind and other new content.

items.title: Video Game Publishers Industry Benchmark Report 2026 Released
items.summary: A new report analyzes the financial performance, sales growth, and market share of the 153 largest video game publishers globally, ranking the top 50 and highlighting trends.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Micron Technology Announces $200 Billion U.S. Buildout Through 2030
items.summary: Micron announced a major $200 billion investment in U.S. manufacturing through 2030, with HBM4 memory shipments arriving ahead of schedule and 2026 output already allocated to customers. S&P also upgraded Micron's credit rating to BBB, citing stronger demand and supporting the company's large capital expenditure plans.

items.title: Netflix Abandons Warner Bros. Discovery Acquisition, Stock Surges 10%
items.summary: Netflix announced it will not match Paramount's $31 per share offer for Warner Bros. Discovery, instead reallocating capital toward a record $20 billion content budget and expanded share repurchase program. The decision sent Netflix shares up nearly 10% to $92 per share, marking the highest level since mid-2025.

items.title: Canada Adds Phosphate to Critical Minerals List
items.summary: Canada's federal government added phosphate to its critical minerals list for clean technology, making phosphate projects eligible for two 30% refundable tax credits. First Phosphate welcomed the amendment, which supports North American lithium iron phosphate battery cell production.

items.title: Supreme Court Tariff Ruling May Provide Refunds for U.S. Coffee Importers
items.summary: A Supreme Court ruling on tariffs could result in potential refunds for U.S. coffee importers and roasters, according to industry news coverage.
